33	// TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins verifies that plain `ddx init` creates
34	// <project>/.ddx/ and the manifest but does not write .ddx/plugins/ddx,
35	// .agents/skills/ddx, .claude/skills/ddx, or any project-local plugins/ tree.
36	func TestInitProject_DoesNotInstallPlugins(t *testing.T) {
37		te := NewTestEnvironment(t, WithGitInit(false))
38		_, err := te.RunCommand("init", "--no-git")
39		require.NoError(t, err)
40	
41		// .ddx/ directory and config.yaml must exist.
42		assert.DirExists(t, filepath.Join(te.Dir, ddxroot.DirName))
43		assert.FileExists(t, filepath.Join(te.Dir, ddxroot.DirName, "config.yaml"))
44	
45		// No project-local plugins/ tree must be created.
46		pluginsDir := filepath.Join(te.Dir, ddxroot.DirName, "plugins")
47		_, statErr := os.Stat(pluginsDir)
48		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(statErr),
49			".ddx/plugins/ must not be created by plain ddx init; got stat err=%v", statErr)
50	
51		// No agent-tier skill links must be installed.
52		for _, surface := range []string{".agents/skills/ddx", ".claude/skills/ddx"} {
53			skillDir := filepath.Join(te.Dir, surface)
54			_, statErr := os.Stat(skillDir)
55			assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(statErr),
56				"%s must not be created by plain ddx init; got stat err=%v", surface, statErr)
57		}
58	}

60	// TestInitProject_LeavesPluginsForLazyResolution verifies that after plain
61	// `ddx init`, registry.ResolvePlugin("ddx") falls through to the baked-in
62	// layer because no project-local or global plugin was installed.
63	func TestInitProject_LeavesPluginsForLazyResolution(t *testing.T) {
64		// Isolate global DDx dir so a pre-existing global install doesn't interfere.
65		xdgDir := t.TempDir()
66		t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDir)
67	
68		te := NewTestEnvironment(t, WithGitInit(false))
69		_, err := te.RunCommand("init", "--no-git")
70		require.NoError(t, err)
71	
72		_, layer, resolveErr := registry.ResolvePlugin(context.Background(), te.Dir, "ddx")
73		require.NoError(t, resolveErr)
74		assert.Equal(t, "baked-in", layer,
75			"ResolvePlugin must fall through to baked-in after plain init (no project-local or global install)")
76	}

78	// TestCleanupBootstrapSkills_RemovesStaleSkills verifies stale ddx-* skills are removed.

83		staleDir := filepath.Join(targetDir, "ddx-stale")
84		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(staleDir, 0o755))

87		// Create a kept skill
88		keepDir := filepath.Join(targetDir, "ddx-doctor")
89		require.NoError(t, os.MkdirAll(keepDir, 0o755))
90		require.NoError(t, os.WriteFile(filepath.Join(keepDir, "SKILL.md"), []byte("# Doctor"), 0o644))
91	
92		cleanupBootstrapSkills(targetDir, []string{"ddx-doctor"})
93	
94		_, err := os.Stat(staleDir)
95		assert.True(t, os.IsNotExist(err), "stale ddx-* skill should be removed")
96		assert.DirExists(t, keepDir, "kept ddx-* skill should remain")
97	}

732	// TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks verifies that `ddx init --global`:
733	//   - installs the default ddx plugin to ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/ddx
734	//   - creates ~/.claude/skills/ddx and ~/.agents/skills/ddx symlinks pointing into it
735	//   - writes ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/config.yaml with convention defaults
736	func TestInitGlobal_CreatesAgentTierLinks(t *testing.T) {
737		xdgDir := t.TempDir()
738		homeDir := t.TempDir()
739		t.Setenv("XDG_DATA_HOME", xdgDir)
740		t.Setenv("HOME", homeDir)
741	
742		te := NewTestEnvironment(t, WithGitInit(false))
743		_, err := te.RunCommand("init", "--global")
744		require.NoError(t, err)
745	
746		globalPluginDir := filepath.Join(xdgDir, "ddx", "global", "plugins", "ddx")
747		expectedTarget := filepath.Join(globalPluginDir, "skills", "ddx")
748	
749		// Plugin must be installed into the global plugin dir.
750		assert.DirExists(t, globalPluginDir, "global plugin dir must be created by ddx init --global")
751	
752		// Agent-tier skill links must exist and point into the global plugin dir.
753		for _, surface := range []string{".claude/skills", ".agents/skills"} {
754			link := filepath.Join(homeDir, surface, "ddx")
755			info, statErr := os.Lstat(link)
756			require.NoError(t, statErr, "skill link %s must exist", link)
757			assert.True(t, info.Mode()&os.ModeSymlink != 0, "%s must be a symlink", link)
758			target, readErr := os.Readlink(link)
759			require.NoError(t, readErr)
760			assert.Equal(t, expectedTarget, target, "%s must point into global plugin dir", link)
761		}
762	
763		// Global config.yaml must be written with convention defaults.
764		configPath := filepath.Join(xdgDir, "ddx", "global", "config.yaml")
765		assert.FileExists(t, configPath, "global config.yaml must be written by ddx init --global")
766		data, readErr := os.ReadFile(configPath)
767		require.NoError(t, readErr)
768		assert.Contains(t, string(data), "version:", "global config.yaml must contain version field")
769	}
770

207:## Persona System
217:Plugin and persona lookup follows a three-layer precedence: project-local (`<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`) → global (`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/`) → baked-in binary default (only for the `ddx` plugin). The project layer always wins when present. See `docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md` for the full decision record.

data.text: Let me read the full Persona System section:

data.output:
207	## Persona System
208	
209	DDX includes a persona system that provides consistent AI personalities for different roles:
210	
211	- **Personas**: Reusable AI personality templates (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`, `architect`)
212	- **Roles**: Abstract functions that personas fulfill (e.g., `code-reviewer`, `test-engineer`)
213	- **Bindings**: Project-specific mappings between roles and personas in `.ddx.yml`
214	
215	Personas enable consistent, high-quality AI interactions across team members and projects. Projects bind specific personas to roles. See `library/personas/` for available personas and `library/personas/README.md` for detailed documentation.
216	
217	Plugin and persona lookup follows a three-layer precedence: project-local (`<project>/.ddx/plugins/<name>/`) → global (`${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/global/plugins/<name>/`) → baked-in binary default (only for the `ddx` plugin). The project layer always wins when present. See `docs/helix/02-design/adr/ADR-027-skill-install-topology.md` for the full decision record.
218	
219	## When filing beads
220	
221	Bead descriptions + AC must be sufficient context for a sub-agent to execute the work without operator hand-curation (principle P7, `docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md`). Before invoking `ddx bead create`, walk the 8-criterion rubric in `docs/helix/06-iterate/bead-authoring-template.md`: title clarity, root cause with file:line, numbered AC with specific test names, wired-in assertion, `cd cli && go test` + lefthook gate, labels with cross-refs, parent + deps, standalone description (no `/tmp/*`, no chat references). Beads scoring < 7/8 will fail in autonomous drain.
222	
223	See docs/helix/06-iterate/reliability-principles.md for the 7 reliability principles applied to ddx try / ddx work execution.
224	
225	## Execution Evidence Convention
226	
227	`.ddx/executions/` is **per-machine working state** — it is listed in `.gitignore` and is NOT committed to git. Files written there by agents (prompt.md, result.json, manifest.json, etc.) exist only on the local machine. The durable audit trail is the events stream in `.ddx/attachments/`, not the execution dirs.
228	
229	When a bead's acceptance criteria asks for an investigation report, findings document, or any other freestanding non-source artifact, the agent must write that artifact under the per-attempt evidence directory `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/` (the `{{.AttemptDir}}` slot in execute-bead prompts). **Never write reports to `/tmp` or any path outside the repository working directory** — such paths are invisible to the post-execution reviewer and do not survive between machines. If the bead names a specific tracked in-repo path for the report, use that path; otherwise default to `.ddx/executions/<run-id>/<short-name>.md` (which will be on-disk but untracked).
230	
231	## When filing beads
